title: RSF Recaptures Bara and Seizes Karnoi; SAF Repels Dilling Assault
detail: RSF forces seized the strategic city of Bara in North Kordofan — the second-largest city in the state and approximately 45 km east of the state capital El Obeid — in a dawn assault deploying dozens of combat vehicles, drones, and artillery. The SAF had recaptured Bara only 10 days earlier. The RSF also seized the border town of Karnoi in North Darfur on the same day. In a separate engagement, the RSF and allied SPLM-N al-Hilu militia assaulted the highway town of Dilling, 100 miles south of El Obeid, but the SAF repelled the attack. The loss of Bara continued the RSF's strategy of encircling El Obeid, which would secure Sudan's central east-west axis and bring the RSF closer to Khartoum.

media.caption: RSF recaptures Bara and seizes Karnoi in North Kordofan

title: OHCHR: 200+ Civilians Killed by Drones Since March 4 in Kordofan and White Nile
detail: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk stated he was appalled by the devastating impact on civilians from increasing drone attacks, noting reports that more than 200 civilians had been killed by drones since March 4 alone in the Kordofan region and White Nile State. OHCHR documented that more than 500 civilians were killed in drone strikes from January 1 to March 15 across Sudan, the vast majority in three Kordofan states. The High Commissioner urged both parties to stop using increasingly powerful drones to deploy explosive weapons in populated areas, warning continued patterns of such attacks may amount to war crimes.

media.caption: OHCHR reports 200+ civilians killed by drones since March 4 in Kordofan and White Nile
